description: Figure 14 is a grouped bar chart illustrating the average Vickers hardness of the cross-sectional (Cross) and longitudinal (Longitudinal) sections of Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni low-density steel fabricated by selective laser melting (SLM) at laser powers of 70 W (P-70), 90 W (P-90), 110 W (P-110), 130 W (P-130), and 150 W (P-150). The vertical axis indicates hardness in HV. For both section orientations, hardness increases with laser power from P-70 to P-110, reaching peak values of 294 HV (longitudinal) and 292 HV (cross-section), and then decreases at P-130 and P-150. The specific recorded values are 279 and 278 HV for P-70, 289 and 285 HV for P-90, 294 and 292 HV for P-110, 284 and 283 HV for P-130, and 283 and 281 HV for P-150, respectively, for longitudinal and cross sections. Error bars represent data variability. The figure indicates that longitudinal sections are consistently slightly harder than cross-sections, and the accompanying text attributes the hardness trend to the combined effects of grain size and internal defects such as pores, cracks, and unfused powder particles.
